_schema: Consolidated research findings. Each section has cards that are expandable in the UI. status: confirmed (we have a document/citation), likely (strong evidence but not yet a primary source in hand), lead (worth chasing).

sections.intro_en: The most consequential discoveries from the 2026-05-21 deep research pass. These are documentary, externally-verifiable links between our family memory and the public historical record.
sections.intro_he: הממצאים החשובים ביותר ממחקר העומק של 21.5.2026 — קישורים מתועדים בין זיכרון המשפחה לרשומה ההיסטורית הציבורית.

cards.title_en: 🎯 David Memek + Lusia's exact death dates + burial CONFIRMED (Haifa CK)
cards.title_he: 🎯 תאריכי הפטירה והקבר של דוד ממק ולוסיה אומתו (חברה קדישא חיפה)
cards.summary_en: Via the Haifa Chevra Kadisha cemetery database, both grandparents' exact death dates + burial locations are now confirmed. David Memek died 29 August 1990 (Hebrew 8 Elul 5750) aged 78; Lusia (Lusha/Leah) died 28 December 1996 (Hebrew 18 Tevet 5757) aged 83. They are buried in adjacent graves at Sde Yehoshua cemetery (formerly Kfar Samir), Haifa — Section ו, Row 22, Graves 102ד (David) + 102ג (Lusia). The match is overwhelmingly verified: Lusia's CK record names her father as אליהו (Eliyahu = Eli Weitzner) and mother as מטילדה (Matilda = Mathilde Weinreb), confirming family identity. Married for ~55-60 years.
cards.summary_he: באמצעות מסד הנתונים של חברה קדישא חיפה, אומתו תאריכי הפטירה והקבורה של שני הסבים. דוד ממק נפטר 29 באוגוסט 1990 (ח׳ אלול תש״ן) בגיל 78; לוסיה נפטרה 28 בדצמבר 1996 (י״ח טבת תשנ״ז) בגיל 83. הם קבורים בקברים סמוכים בבית העלמין שדה יהושע (לשעבר כפר סמיר), חיפה — חלקה ו, שורה 22, מקום 102ד (דוד) + 102ג (לוסיה). זיהוי החוצה מאומת על-ידי שמות ההורים של לוסיה: אביה אליהו (אלי וייצנר) ואמה מטילדה (מתילדה ויינרב).

cards.title_en: 🎯 The two halves of Doron's family knew each other in pre-WWII Galicia
cards.title_he: 🎯 שני חצאי משפחת דורון הכירו זה את זה בגליציה לפני מלחמת העולם השנייה
cards.summary_en: Per family stories: Doron's maternal grandmother ESTER GOLDFISCHER studied with FEIGE (Tzipora) WEITZNER — Lusia's older sister, born 1911 Bolechów — almost certainly at a Bolechów-area Hebrew or vocational school in the late 1920s / early 1930s. Combined with the geographic proximity (S. Goldfischer was born in Skole, ~60km from Bolechów, in the same Galician Carpathian region as Bolechów + Stryj + Nadwórna), this means the WEITZNER and GOLDFISCHER families moved in the same small Galician Jewish circle decades before Dov Rapaport (David + Lusia's son) and Dalia Goldfischer (Ester + S. Goldfischer's daughter) ever met or married. The two halves of Doron's family had a documented pre-Holocaust connection. Research into the specific school is in progress.

cards.title_en: ✅ CONFIRMED — The Rapaports ARE Kohanim (priestly descent)
cards.title_he: ✅ מאומת — משפחת רפפורט היא כהנים (מצאצאי הכוהן הגדול)
cards.summary_en: Family tradition confirms (May 2026): the Rapaport family IS Kohanim — descendants of the ancient priestly line. The surname "Rapaport" (Rapa-Porto) is itself the Kohanic identifier, carried by one of the most famous Ashkenazi rabbinical dynasties. The family does not go by "Cohen" as a modern surname — "Rapaport" IS the Kohen-line surname. Documented dynasty: from R. Meshullam Yekutiel ha-Kohen Rapa (d.1450, originally Mainz then Porto, Italy), through the Galician branch via R. Abraham Rapoport "Schrenzel" (Lemberg 1584-1651, head of Lemberg yeshiva 45 years, son-in-law of R. Mordecai Schrenzel, President of the Council of Four Lands). Our Berisz Rapaport of Nadwórna sits at the modern end of this dynasty (4-6 generations from the named rabbis). This connects David Memek's line to ~600 years of documented Kohen rabbinical descent. ALSO CONFIRMED by independent third-party documentation: (a) the Jewish Community of Porto officially recognized the family as Sephardic-Kohen descent in December 2019, qualifying Doron for Portuguese citizenship; (b) Dr Chanan Rapaport's 2018 paper established the Sephardic origin with 19 medieval Spanish archival references + mitochondrial DNA evidence; (c) the iconic family emblem (raven + priestly blessing hands) was published in Verona 1594 in R. Avraham Menachem Rapa-Porto's 'Mincha Belulah' and still appears on this archive's website header.
cards.summary_he: מסורת המשפחה (מאי 2026) מאשרת: משפחת רפפורט היא כהנים — מצאצאי הכהונה הקדומה. השם 'רפפורט' (רפא-פורטו) עצמו הוא הסימן הכהני, נישא על-ידי אחת מהשושלות הרבניות האשכנזיות המפורסמות. המשפחה לא נושאת את השם 'כהן' כשם משפחה מודרני — 'רפפורט' הוא שם המשפחה של קו הכהונה. השושלת מתועדת: מהרב משולם יקותיאל הכהן רפא (נפטר 1450, במקור ממיינץ ואז מפורטו, איטליה), דרך ענף גליציה של הרב אברהם רפפורט 'שרנצל' (לבוב 1584-1651, ראש ישיבת לבוב 45 שנה). בריש רפפורט הוא הקצה המודרני של השושלת. מקשר את שושלת דוד ממק ל-600 שנות יוחסין כהני-רבני.
cards.source: Wikipedia: Rapoport family; Jewish Encyclopedia: Rapoport
cards.quote_en: The Rapoport family (Hebrew: רפופורט, also spelled Rapaport, Rappoport, Rappaport) is one of the most famous Ashkenazi Jewish rabbinical families, dating back to the 15th century. Family members claim Kohanic descent. The progenitor of the family was Rabbi Yekutiel (Meshullam Mendel) ha-Kohen Rapa, who settled in Porto.

cards.title_en: 1957 Bolechów Yizkor names "D. Rapaport, Eli Weitzner's son-in-law"
cards.title_he: ספר היזכור של בולחוב 1957 — "ד. רפפורט, חתנו של אלי וייצנר"
cards.summary_en: The Bolechów memorial book published in 1957 directly names D. Rapaport as Eli Weitzner's son-in-law and a Revisionist Zionist organiser in Bolechów. This is documentary, third-party confirmation of David's marriage to a Weitzner daughter and his Betar/Revisionist activism — printed 69 years ago.
cards.summary_he: ספר היזכור של בולחוב שיצא ב-1957 קורא במפורש לד. רפפורט בשם 'חתנו של אלי וייצנר' ופעיל רוויזיוניסטי בבולחוב. תיעוד מ-1957, אישור עצמאי שדוד נישא לבת וייצנר ושהיה פעיל בית"רי.
cards.quote_en: The Revisionist ranks were organized as follows: adults in "Hatza'r", the young workers and students in "Massadah". The youngest were sent to "Betar". Thanks to comrade D. Rapaport (Eli Weitzner's son-in-law) the movement grew. They had interesting programs and summer camps that were also used for military exercises (along the model of P. W.). Besides developing cultural activities, there were also Hebrew classes under the direction of Hinde Delman.
cards.source: Sefer ha-zikaron le-kedoshei Bolechow (Memorial Book of the Martyrs of Bolechow), Y. Eshel ed., 1957 — Yiddish page 278, "Memories" by Abraham Weber, translated by Judie Goldstein

cards.title_en: Rebeka Griffel b.1888 = direct sister of Dawid Mendel Griffel (Nadwórna timber-and-oil magnate)
cards.title_he: רבקה גריפל ילידת 1888 = אחות ישירה של דוד מנדל גריפל (איל עץ ונפט מנדבורנה)
cards.summary_en: The Geni profile of Dawid Mendel Griffel (1875-1941) explicitly lists "Rebeka Rapaport" as his sister. Their father was Eliezer Griffel (1850-1918), head of the Nadwórna Jewish community and a major timber-and-oil businessman. Their mother was Sarah Matel Chajes of Kolomea, of the Chajes rabbinical dynasty. David "Memek" Rapaport (b.1911-12-25) was named after his maternal uncle. The family is therefore documented part of the extended Gelles-Griffel-Chajes-Wahl Galician dynasty, researched in depth by Edward Gelles at Balliol College, Oxford.
cards.summary_he: פרופיל ג'יני של דוד מנדל גריפל (1875-1941) מציין במפורש את 'רבקה רפפורט' כאחותו. אביהם היה אליעזר גריפל (1850-1918), ראש קהילת נדבורנה ובעל עסקי עץ ונפט. אמם הייתה שרה מאטל חיות מקולומיאה, מאילן היוחסין הרבני של החיות. דוד 'ממק' רפפורט (יליד 25.12.1911) נקרא על שם דודו דוד מנדל גריפל.
cards.source: Geni.com + Edward Gelles, "Griffel of Nadworna" (Balliol College Oxford archives)

cards.title_en: Nadwórna hosted the FIRST Beitar hachshara (training farm) in Poland — 1929
cards.title_he: נדבורנה הייתה בית ההכשרה הראשון של בית"ר בפולין — 1929
cards.summary_en: Yad Vashem's Valley of the Communities entry for Nadwórna states: "The first Beitar hachshara (training farm) in Poland was established in Nadwórna in 1929." David Rapaport was 17-18 at the founding — almost certainly a founding member.
cards.summary_he: ערך 'עמק הקהילות' של יד ושם על נדבורנה: 'בית ההכשרה הראשון של בית"ר בפולין הוקם בנדבורנה ב-1929'. דוד היה אז בן 17-18 — כמעט בוודאות חבר מייסד.
cards.source: Yad Vashem — Valley of the Communities, Nadwórna

cards.title_en: The Nadwórna timber industry was Jewish-owned — Foresta + Zetperol
cards.title_he: תעשיית העץ של נדבורנה הייתה בבעלות יהודית — חברות פורסטה וזטפרול
cards.summary_en: Pre-war Nadwórna had two major Jewish-owned timber operations: "Foresta" leased state woodlands and exported timber (all owners and workers Jewish; contract cancelled 1937 under antisemitic pressure), and "Zetperol" (early 1920s) supplied packing-crate timber. Under German occupation 1941-42, ~600 Jews worked daily at the Nadwórna sawmill — David was one of them.
cards.summary_he: לפני המלחמה היו בנדבורנה שני מפעלי עץ יהודיים גדולים: 'פורסטה' (חכירת יערות מהמדינה הפולנית — בעלים ועובדים יהודים, החוזה בוטל ב-1937 בלחץ אנטישמי) ו'זטפרול' (תחילת שנות העשרים — עץ לאריזות יצוא). תחת הכיבוש הגרמני 1941-42 כ-600 יהודים עבדו במנסרת נדבורנה — דוד היה אחד מהם.
cards.source: Yad Vashem + Pinkas Hakehillot Polin: Nadvirna (JewishGen)
cards.quote_en: The 'Foresta' company was active in the city, leasing woodlands from the Polish authorities in order to chop timber and prepare it for export. Both the managers of the company in the city and its workers were Jewish… In 1937, the government contract with the 'Foresta' company – whose owners and workers in Nadwórna were Jewish – was terminated, due to intervention by antisemitic Ukrainians and Poles. The company's Jewish employees were fired. … Some 600 Jews worked daily at the saw mill (during the German occupation).

cards.title_en: The Nadwórna sawmill where David worked was likely Aryanised GLESINGER timber property
cards.title_he: מנסרת נדבורנה שבה עבד דוד הייתה ככל הנראה רכוש משפחת גלסינגר שעוּבּר לארים
cards.summary_en: Egon Glesinger (1905-1979) was a Galician Jewish forestry magnate (doctorate "Le bois en Europe" 1931 Geneva, author of "Nazis in the Woodpile" 1942). His family conglomerate owned much of the Galician timber industry pre-war. The Nadwórna sawmill where David worked under Nazi occupation was very likely a Glesinger property Aryanised in 1941 under Treuhänder administration. Confirmable via Bundesarchiv R187 Treuhandstelle or Galizien Treuhand records at Bundesarchiv Berlin Lichterfelde R-series.
cards.source: WikiTree biography + Glesinger 1942 book "Nazis in the Woodpile"

cards.title_en: 📜 Eric Griffel ADST Oral History (2015) confirms Griffel-Nadwórna timber dynasty
cards.title_he: 📜 היסטוריה בעל-פה של אריק גריפל (2015) — מאשרת את שושלת העץ של משפחת גריפל מנדבורנה
cards.summary_en: Eric Griffel (1930 Krakow – 2020 Rockville MD), David Memek Rapaport's first cousin, gave a 205-page oral history to ADST (Association for Diplomatic Studies and Training) in 2015. He directly confirms: (a) his father Zygmunt Griffel was born near Nadwórna and is buried on Staten Island in the Nadwórna Jewish émigré community organization plot; (b) Zygmunt's timber business — developing tracts of forest and exporting product mainly to England; (c) his uncle Edward Griffel set up business in England before WWII; (d) Eric's mother Maryla née Susser was from a Krakow rabbinical-banking-jewelry family. Holocaust loss anchor: 'Maryla's mother and sister were shot by the Nazis in our apartment in Lwów.' Post-war Polish Government claims: 'three houses in Krakow and an interest in a bank on the central square in Krakow.' Zygmunt also had German reparation claims for 'confiscation of specialized timber in Poland.'
cards.source: Eric Griffel, ADST Oral History 'A Non-Forgiving Life', 2015. PDF cached locally at docs/research/family_documents/eric_griffel_adst_oral_history_2015.pdf

cards.title_en: 🏕️ The Nadwórna Beitar hachshara was called 'Kibbutz Achwa' (אחווה / fraternity)
cards.title_he: 🏕️ הכשרת בית"ר הראשונה בפולין נקראה 'קיבוץ אחווה' בנדבורנה
cards.summary_en: Yad Vashem photo archive 9933470 — a 1936 photograph of Leksio Hoffmann at 'Achwa' Hachshara Kibbutz in Nadwórna — confirms the kibbutz name. Dedication on back: 'as eternal souvenir from our short stay in the Kibbutz, from me, Leksio Hoffmann, Nadworna, August 9, 1936.' The 1929-founded first Polish Beitar hachshara was still operating in August 1936 as 'Kibbutz Achwa'. David Rapaport was 17-18 in 1929 (founding year) and 24-25 in 1936 — very likely appears in any Achwa group photograph.

cards.title_en: 🚢 Theodor Herzl voyage details — Sète 2 April 1947, 2,641 passengers, 3 killed
cards.summary_en: Confirmed via Palmach/Palyam ship history: Sailed from Sète, France, on 2 April 1947 carrying 2,641 Ma'apilim drawn from camps in France and Belgium. Commander Mecca Limon; officers Yosh Halvei, Betzalel 'Tzolo' Feldman, Chaim Wineshelboim; Gideoni Nachman 'Bob' Burstein. Intercepted 13 April 1947 by HMS Haydon and HMS St Brides Bay. Three Ma'apilim killed by British gunfire: Aharon Dov, Pinchas Weiss, Menachem Samet. 27 wounded. Passengers sent to Cyprus detention camps (Karaolos near Famagusta) for 8 months. Note: William Bernstein, sometimes confused with the Herzl, was killed on the SS Exodus — NOT the Theodor Herzl.
cards.quote_en: On 13 April 1947, the Theodor Herzl (2,641 passengers) was intercepted by HMS Haydon and HMS St Brides Bay. Passengers resisted heavily; three were killed and 27 were injured.
cards.source: Palmach/Palyam — 'The Voyage of the Theodor Herzl'

sections.intro_en: The Rapaport family's true ancestral origin: medieval Sephardic Spain, NOT Italian-Ashkenazi as tradition believed. Dr Chanan Rapaport (b.1928, Director of the Center for the Study of the Rapaport Family) established this in his foundational 2018 paper. The earliest documented ancestors are Dr Vidal Rapapa (1305 Mallorca) and Dr Jucef Salomon Rapapa (1311 Mallorca, court physician to King Jaime III). After the 1391 pogroms and the 1492 Spanish expulsion, the family fled to Italy where Isaac 'HaMoel' ben Yechiel Michael ha-Kohen Rapa first used the surname 'Rapa-Porto' ~1550. This Sephardic origin was OFFICIALLY CERTIFIED in December 2019 by the Jewish Community of Porto, qualifying Doron and family for Portuguese citizenship by Sephardic descent.
sections.intro_he: המוצא האבותי האמיתי של משפחת רפפורט: ספרד הספרדית של ימי הביניים, ולא איטליה האשכנזית כפי שהאמינה המסורת. ד"ר חנן רפפורט (יליד 1928, מנהל המרכז לחקר משפחת רפפורט) ביסס זאת במאמר היסודי שלו מ-2018. אבות מתועדים מוקדמים: ד"ר וידאל רפפא (1305 מיורקה) וד"ר יוסף שלמה רפפא (1311 מיורקה, רופא חצר של המלך ז'איימה השלישי). לאחר פוגרומי 1391 וגירוש 1492, המשפחה ברחה לאיטליה. המוצא הספרדי אומת רשמית בדצמבר 2019 על-ידי הקהילה היהודית של פורטו, כשהכשירה את דורון ומשפחתו לאזרחות פורטוגלית לפי מוצא ספרדי.

cards.title_en: ✅ Jewish Community of Porto APPROVAL of Sephardic descent (December 2019)
cards.title_he: ✅ אישור הקהילה היהודית של פורטו על מוצא ספרדי (דצמבר 2019)
cards.summary_en: On 3 December 2019, the Nationality Law Committee of the Jewish Community of Porto (Kadoorie Mekor Haim Synagogue) APPROVED Doron Rapaport's application for certification as a Sephardic descendant qualifying for Portuguese nationality. The application package included: birth certificates, Israeli ID, family tree, and the Dr Chanan Rapaport 2018 research paper as proof of judaism + Sephardic origin. The Porto Community digitally-signed certificate is a Portuguese-State-recognized proof of the family's Sephardic descent — a legal third-party confirmation that the Rapaports are descendants of Spanish/Portuguese Jews expelled in 1492.
cards.summary_he: ב-3 בדצמבר 2019, ועדת חוק האזרחות של הקהילה היהודית של פורטו (בית כנסת קדורי מקור חיים) אישרה את בקשתו של דורון רפפורט לאישור כצאצא ספרדי הזכאי לאזרחות פורטוגלית. חבילת הבקשה כללה: תעודות לידה, ת"ז ישראלית, אילן יוחסין, ומאמר המחקר של ד"ר חנן רפפורט מ-2018.
cards.quote_en: Shalom. Approved. — Nationality Law Committee, Jewish Community of Oporto (Kadoorie Mekor Haim Synagogue, Rua de Guerra Junqueiro 340, Porto, Portugal)
cards.source: Email correspondence Nov-Dec 2019, Jewish Community of Porto

cards.title_en: Dr Chanan Rapaport's foundational 2018 paper (Israel Genealogy Research Association)
cards.title_he: מאמר היסוד של ד"ר חנן רפפורט 2018 (אגודת חקר הגנאלוגיה של ישראל)
cards.summary_en: Dr Chanan Rapaport (b.1928, Director of the Center for the Study of the Rapaport Family; board member of the International Institute for Jewish Genealogy at the National Library Jerusalem) published this foundational paper on 24 April 2018, demonstrating with multiple independent evidence streams that the Rapaport family originated in medieval Sephardic Spain — not in Ashkenazi central Europe as commonly believed. Evidence includes: (a) 19 archival references to Rapapa / Rap / Rab Jewish individuals in Mallorca/Aragon/Catalonia/Navarra 1250-1492 (compiled by Sephardic genealogist Matilda Tager); (b) mitochondrial-DNA match between US Rappoport and Mallorca Chueta family, both showing Levantine-Sephardic haplogroup; (c) the etymology RAP A la PortA = 'predator at the gate', Spanish/Catalan; (d) the 1305 Dr Vidal Rapapa court records, and the 1345 Dr Jucef Salomon Rapapa lawsuit against King Pedro IV for 10 Libres (≈ €150,000-€600,000 today).
cards.summary_he: ד"ר חנן רפפורט פרסם ב-24 באפריל 2018 את המאמר היסודי המוכיח שמשפחת רפפורט מקורה בספרד הספרדית של ימי הביניים — לא במרכז אירופה האשכנזית כפי שמקובל היה לחשוב. הראיות כוללות 19 רשומות ארכיוניות של רפפא במיורקה/אראגון/קטלוניה/נווארה 1250-1492, התאמת DNA מיטוכונדריאלי בין רפופורט אמריקאי לצ'ואטה ממיורקה, ותיעוד רפואי של ד"ר וידאל רפפא 1305 וד"ר יוסף שלמה רפפא 1345.
cards.quote_en: I came looking for the origins of a known and respectable Ashkenazi family and I found them! ... The conclusion that emerged from these facts is that the origin of this tribe and its many families is in territories ruled by the Spanish kingdoms, i.e., the origin of this most-known Jewish Ashkenazi family Rappaport is in Spain.
cards.source: Dr Chanan Rapaport, Israel Genealogy Research Association, 24 April 2018. PDF locally cached at docs/research/family_documents/chanan_rapaport_historia_2018.pdf

cards.title_en: Dr Vidal Rapapa (1305 Mallorca) — earliest documented ancestor by name
cards.title_he: ד"ר וידאל רפפא (1305 מיורקה) — האב הקדמון המתועד הראשון בשם
cards.summary_en: Physician of Mallorca. In 1305 he led a group of Mallorcan Jews — including Aahron, Maimon ben Estruc Ibn-Nunu, David ben Sopran, and Ensrum of Suria (Catalonia) — who conspired to rescue a young Jewish woman from her planned conversion-by-marriage to a Christian. The plot was uncovered by Spanish Church investigators; the group was sentenced to long imprisonment + heavy fines, then King Jaime II reduced the sentences in exchange for Dr. Vidal Rapapa's secrecy. Documented in Antonio Pons, 'Los Judíos del Reino de Mallorca durante los siglos XIII y XIV', v.2 p.29.
cards.source: Antonio Pons, Los Judíos del Reino de Mallorca, v.2 p.29; Dr Chanan Rapaport 2018

cards.title_en: Dr Jucef Salomon Rapapa — court physician to King Jaime III of Mallorca
cards.title_he: ד"ר יוסף שלמה רפפא — רופא חצר של המלך ז'איימה השלישי
cards.summary_en: Court physician (Physicus) to King Jaime III of Mallorca. In 1345 he filed suit against the successor King Pedro IV for non-payment of 10 Libres for medical services rendered to King Jaime III and his entourage. Dr Chanan Rapaport's analysis (consulting Prof. Jeff Malka and citing artist Bonaventura Perpinya's 1309 contract with King Sancho I) establishes that 1 Libra in 1345 ≈ €60,000-€150,000 today — so 10 Libres ≈ €600,000-€1.5 million in modern purchasing power. Documented in Contreras Mas Antonio, 'Médicos Judíos en Mallorca durante la Edad Media' (Palma de Mallorca, 1977) p.131.
cards.source: Contreras Mas Antonio, Médicos Judíos en Mallorca, 1977, p.131

cards.title_en: Isaac 'HaMoel' Rapa-Porto — first to use the 'Rapa-Porto' surname (~1550, Italy)
cards.title_he: יצחק 'המוהל' רפא-פורטו — הראשון להשתמש בשם 'רפא-פורטו' (~1550, איטליה)
cards.summary_en: Son of R. Yechiel Michael ha-Kohen Rapa (b.1502 Portobuffolè). After the family fled Spanish persecution → Mainz → second expulsion from Mainz → through the Italian Adige river ports (Legnago, Mestre, Venice, Portobuffolè, Piove di Sacco), Isaac 'HaMoel' was the first family member to add 'Porto' to the surname. From him spread the tradition that created the modern surname Rapa-Porto = Rapoport. Documented by researcher Dr Daniel Nissim, cited in Dr Chanan Rapaport's paper.
cards.source: Dr Daniel Nissim, cited in Chanan Rapaport 2018

cards.title_en: 📖 The 1594 Verona family emblem (raven + priestly blessing) — the family symbol
cards.title_he: 📖 סמל המשפחה מוורונה 1594 (עורב + ברכת כהנים) — סמל המשפחה
cards.summary_en: Rabbi Dr Avraham Menachem ha-Kohen Rapa-Porto (1520-1596) published in Verona in 1594 the important rabbinical work 'Mincha Belulah', where he referred to himself as 'Min HaOrvim' (of the ravens). The book's printer's emblem — a raven at the center, flanked by two upraised palms in the configuration of the 'Birkat Kohanim' (Priestly Blessing) — became the iconic family symbol. The SAME emblem appears on Doron's family coat of arms displayed on this archive's website header. It also matches the raven on the Galicia and Lodomeria coat of arms (Austria-Hungary).
cards.quote_en: The central part, flanked by two bare-breasted women, depicts two open palms in the well-known 'Priestly Blessing' over the family symbol, the raven.
cards.source: R. Avraham Menachem Rapa, Mincha Belulah (Verona 1594); analysed in Dr. Michael K. Silber's Seforim Blog (Hebrew University)

cards.title_en: 👑 Baron Dr Arnold Rapoport-Adler Von Porada (Galicia, MP, granted Baron 1890)
cards.title_he: 👑 הברון ד"ר ארנולד רפפורט-אדלר פון פוראדה (גליציה, ח"כ, ברון 1890)
cards.summary_en: Son of David, son of Shlomoh Yehudah Rapaport (the 'SHI"R'), Chief Rabbi of Prague. Dr Juris (Krakow 1863), then Economics + Political Science at Vienna. Represented the Galician + Lodomerian Jews in the Austro-Hungarian Parliament. On 6 September 1890 Emperor Franz Joseph conferred the title of Baron — "Baron Dr Arnold Rapoport-Adler Von Porada". The name 'Von Porada' is an acronym: POrt-RApa-DAvid (his father's name). Also a Knight of the French Legion of Honour. His coat of arms displays TWO ravens: the central one for the Rapaport family, the upper one for the Kingdom of Galicia (whose own coat of arms also features a raven).
cards.source: Central Archives of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, Vienna; quoted in Chanan Rapaport 2018

cards.title_en: 🧬 DNA evidence — Mallorcan Chueta ↔ US Rappoport mitochondrial match
cards.title_he: 🧬 ראיית DNA — התאמה מיטוכונדריאלית בין צ'ואטה מיורקני לרפופורט אמריקאי
cards.summary_en: A member of the Rappoport family in the US and a member of a Chueta family in Mallorca were tested in a scientific laboratory. The mitochondrial DNA test revealed a direct and precise connection between two Ashkenazi people. It showed clearly that their haplogroup is NOT Spanish, but originates from the Levant — the eastern Mediterranean — and is ancient (like Jews of Eretz-Yisrael before the expulsions). They were brothers — going back 500 years — despite the chasm of vast oceans of geography and history.
cards.quote_en: The mitochondrial DNA test revealed a direct and precise connection between two Ashkenazi people.
cards.source: Dr Chanan Rapaport 2018, citing private DNA test

sections.intro_en: A chronological tour through every place named in Lusia's memoir, with photos (Wikimedia Commons CC-licensed + Jewish Galicia & Bukovina). Each card cites the specific memoir page numbers.
sections.intro_he: מסע כרונולוגי דרך כל מקום שנזכר ביומן של לוסיה, עם תצלומים. כל כרטיס מציין את מספרי העמודים הספציפיים ביומן.

cards.title_en: 🏛️ Nadwórna (Nadvirna) — David Memek's birthplace, 1911  [memoir ch.1]
cards.title_he: 🏛️ נדבורנה — מקום הולדתו של דוד ממק, 1911  [פרק 1 ביומן]
cards.summary_en: David Mendel 'Memek' Rapaport was born 25 December 1911 in Nadwórna, eastern Galicia. His father Berisz/Bernard Rapaport was a merchant; his mother Rebeka Griffel was the 8th of 10 children of Eliezer 'Zeida' Griffel, head of the Nadwórna Jewish community + owner of the town's timber and oil enterprises. The town was on 6 October 1941 the site of the first mass aktion at the Bukowinka Forest, where >2,000 Nadwórna Jews — almost certainly including David's parents Berisz + Rebeka — were murdered. Today: Nadvirna, Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast, Ukraine.

images.caption_en: Nadwórna — the historic Town Hall on Hetman Mazepa Street.
Nadwórna — historic hotel building.

cards.title_en: 🏛️ Bolechów (Bolekhiv) — Lusia's birthplace, 1913  [memoir ch.1]
cards.title_he: 🏛️ בולחוב — מקום הולדתה של לוסיה, 1913  [פרק 1 ביומן]
cards.summary_en: Lusia (Leah) Weitzner was born 8 April 1913 in Bolechów (Bolechów Ruski). Her father Eli/Eliyahu Weitzner started as a cattle dealer and became a tannery owner — tanning was the dominant Jewish industry in Bolechów (in 1874 tannery owner Israel Hauptman was elected mayor). The 1957 Bolechów Yizkor names 'D. Rapaport, Eli Weitzner's son-in-law' — documentary print confirmation of David + Lusia's marriage. Today: Bolekhiv, Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast, Ukraine. FAMILY CONNECTION: per family stories, Lusia's older sister Feige (Tzipora) Weitzner studied here with Ester Goldfischer — Doron's maternal grandmother.

images.caption_en: The Great Synagogue of Bolechów — northern side.
Bolechów town map, 1914.
Bolechów market square (Rynek), northern side.
Bolechów Town Hall.
Bolechów Greek Catholic Church.
Bolechów Forestry Lyceum.
Halicka Street — the Tarbut Hebrew school. Possibly where Ester Goldfischer + Feige Weitzner studied together.
Bolechów Jewish school building.

cards.title_en: 🏞️ Skole — Doron's maternal grandfather S. Goldfischer's birthplace, 1909  [family docs]
cards.title_he: 🏞️ סקולה — מקום הולדתו של ש. גולדפישר (סבא מצד אמא של דורון), 1909  [מסמכים משפחתיים]
cards.summary_en: S. Goldfischer was born 23 November 1909 in Skole — a small town in the Carpathian foothills of eastern Galicia (today Skole, Lviv Oblast, Ukraine). Skole is only ~60km from Bolechów + ~80km from Stryj — the same small Jewish world as the Weitzner and Rapaport families. He was a 'Marin' (sailor / merchant marine) by profession, eventually settling in Haifa. Married Ester Goldfischer. Their daughter Dalia married Dov Rapaport, son of David Memek + Lusia. THE TWO HALVES OF DORON'S FAMILY CONVERGED ON HAIFA from the same Galician Jewish region.
cards.source: Family Israeli ID + French-Hebrew bilingual passport documents (2026)

cards.title_en: 🏨 Muszyna — Pensjonat Bristol, the resort hotel Lusia managed ~1933-1939  [memoir ch.2 pp.16-22]
cards.title_he: 🏨 מושינה — פנסיון בריסטול, המלון שלוסיה ניהלה כ-1933-1939  [פרק 2 ביומן עמ' 16-22]
cards.summary_en: Muszyna is a small resort town in the Beskid mountains of southern Poland, near the Slovak border, ~12km from Krynica-Zdrój. It became an officially-recognised spa town in 1929-30 after mineral-spring development. Lusia rented and managed a fine resort hotel here — almost certainly the famous Jewish-owned 'Pensjonat Bristol', operated by the family of Chaim Weiss (the dominant pre-war Jewish hotelier in Muszyna). Memek (David Rapaport) became her regular boyfriend there; they married in Muszyna around 1934-1936. A standalone 6-page article on the Bristol exists in Almanach Muszyny 1998 (Żebrowski).
cards.source: Wirtualny Sztetl Muszyna + Almanach Muszyny 1998 + Memoir ch.2

images.caption_en: Muszyna Town Hall on the Market Square. The Reich family hotel was on the Rynek.
Panorama of Muszyna in the Beskid mountains.
Pijalnia Anna — Muszyna's mineral water pavilion.
Promenades + sidewalks in Muszyna, with Muszyna Castle background.

cards.title_en: 💀 The Nadwórna sawmill — David's escape from the 24 Oct 1942 liquidation  [memoir pp.25-32, 41-42]
cards.summary_en: Under Nazi occupation 1941-1942, the Nadwórna sawmill (the Jewish-owned 'Foresta' operation) employed ~600 Jews who held German 'essential worker' badges. David Rapaport was one of them — his badge let him leave the ghetto. On 24 October 1942, during the final Nadwórna ghetto liquidation, David and 17 other Jews escaped by hiding inside wood-transport train wagons that he had drilled escape-holes into. Lusia smuggled their son Shimon (then 5) out of the ghetto in a modified vegetable crate.
cards.source: Memoir pp.25-32, 41-42 + Yad Vashem + Pinkas Polin

cards.title_en: 🏠 Lwów: Legionów 24 — Lusia's wartime apartment, opposite the Opera, 1942-1944  [memoir pp.40, 57]
cards.title_he: 🏠 לבוב: לגיונוב 24 — דירת לוסיה בזמן המלחמה, מול האופרה  [יומן עמ' 40, 57]
cards.summary_en: Lusia lived in Lwów (Lviv) on the Aryan side under her false identity 'Maria Cizlik' — a real Nadwórna-born nurse whose birth certificate Lusia used. Her apartment was Legionów Street 24, opposite the city Opera and opposite a German military base from which gunfire was frequent. BUILDING IDENTIFIED 2026-05-21: today's Prospekt Svobody 24, Lviv — a four-storey Biedermeier-style stone kamienica built 1836-1837 by architect Johann Salzmann for F. Adamski. Polish national poet Wincenty Pol lived there 1856-1866. Pre-war: ground floor was a confectionery shop; upper floors were rented apartments — perfect cover. During 1941-44 the street's German name was Adolf-Hitler-Ring (Lemberg's main Nazi-administrative boulevard). Today: Prospekt Svobody, Lviv's central boulevard.

cards.title_en: 🏪 Lwów: the art-objects shop on Rynek Square — Lusia's wartime cover  [memoir pp.43-50]
cards.title_he: 🏪 לבוב: חנות חפצי האמנות בכיכר הרינק — הכיסוי של לוסיה  [יומן עמ' 43-50]
cards.summary_en: Lusia managed an art-objects shop in the commercial complex on Rynek Square (the Old Market Square) in Lwów. The financial manager of the whole complex was a German named 'Doner'. Lusia made buying trips for art objects. The shop's display window was famously beautiful. She passed multiple Gestapo inspections of her false papers there. Today's Площа Ринок in central Lviv — a UNESCO World Heritage Site preserving the medieval merchant town.

cards.title_en: 🇧🇪 Brussels, April 1946 — refugee transit + Dov's birth  [memoir p.62]
cards.title_he: 🇧🇪 בריסל, אפריל 1946 — תחנת הפליטים והולדת דב  [יומן עמ' 62]
cards.summary_en: After the war's end David, Lusia and Shimon left Lwów, transited Katowice (Poland), and reached Brussels in April 1946. The Jewish relief organisation Joint (JDC) paid their hotel rent. Their second son Dov 'Bernard' Rapaport was born in Brussels in 1946 — Doron's father. They remained ~1 year before sailing for Palestine on the Theodor Herzl in April 1947. The Belgian Aliens Police kept individual dossiers on every adult foreigner — guaranteed to exist for David, Lusia, and Dov at the Archives générales du Royaume.
cards.source: Memoir p.62 + Archives générales du Royaume

cards.title_en: 🚢 Sète → Theodor Herzl ship, 2 April 1947 — 2,641 ma'apilim  [memoir pp.63-64]
cards.title_he: 🚢 סט → אוניית תיאודור הרצל, 2 באפריל 1947  [יומן עמ' 63-64]
cards.summary_en: On 2 April 1947 the ship Theodor Herzl sailed from Sète, France, carrying 2,641 Holocaust-survivor immigrants drawn from camps in France and Belgium. David, Lusia, Shimon and infant Dov were aboard. Intercepted 13 April 1947 by HMS Haydon and HMS St Brides Bay. Three Ma'apilim killed by British gunfire (Aharon Dov, Pinchas Weiss, Menachem Samet); 27 wounded. All passengers deported to Cyprus.

cards.title_en: 🏕️ Cyprus — Karaolos internment camp, 1947-1948  [memoir pp.64-65]
cards.title_he: 🏕️ קפריסין — מחנה המעצר קראולוס, 1947-1948  [יומן עמ' 64-65]
cards.summary_en: After British interception, passengers were taken to the Cyprus internment camps (Karaolos near Famagusta + Dekhelia near Larnaca). 12 camps total; held 53,510 Jews 1946-1949; ~2,000 children born in captivity. David's family was held ~8 months. Wounded passengers + unaccompanied minors were diverted to Atlit camp in British Mandate Palestine instead of Cyprus.
cards.source: Memoir pp.64-65 + Wikipedia Cyprus internment camps

cards.title_en: 🇮🇱 Atlit detention camp — Shimon's release document  [memoir p.65]
cards.summary_en: Atlit detainee camp on the coast south of Haifa was the British detention site in Mandate Palestine for intercepted Ma'apilim 1939-1948. The memoir (p.65) references Shimon's release document from Atlit — suggesting young Shimon was separated from his parents (possibly with the wounded from the Theodor Herzl) and held at Atlit while David + Lusia were in Cyprus.
cards.source: Memoir p.65 + Atlit Detainee Camp Heritage Site

cards.title_en: 🏠 Haifa, Moriah Street 93 — the family home, 1948-1990s  [memoir p.70]
cards.title_he: 🏠 חיפה, רחוב מוריה 93 — בית המשפחה, 1948-1990  [יומן עמ' 70]
cards.summary_en: After their release from Cyprus, David and Lusia settled in Haifa. Using German Wiedergutmachung reparations payments, they purchased an apartment of two rooms + kitchen + toilet on Moriah Street 93, on the Carmel ridge in Haifa. This was the family home for the rest of their lives. Dov, Doron, Dana, and Daniel's father grew up in this Haifa milieu — a Polish-speaking, Hebrew-adopting, Galician-survivor household. David Memek died here on 29 August 1990 aged 78. Lusia followed on 28 December 1996 aged 83.

cards.title_en: 🕯️ Sde Yehoshua cemetery, Haifa — David + Lusia's adjacent graves
cards.title_he: 🕯️ בית העלמין שדה יהושע, חיפה — קברי דוד ולוסיה הצמודים
cards.summary_en: Final resting place of David Memek (d.29 Aug 1990) + Lusia (d.28 Dec 1996). Sde Yehoshua cemetery (formerly Kfar Samir), operated by the Haifa Chevra Kadisha. Both buried at Section ו (Vav), Row 22 — David at Grave 102ד, Lusia in the immediately adjacent Grave 102ג, the standard Israeli husband-and-wife burial pattern. Their adjacent graves end this chronological tour where their shared life ended.
cards.summary_he: המקום האחרון של דוד ממק (נפטר 29.8.1990) ולוסיה (נפטרה 28.12.1996). בית העלמין שדה יהושע (לשעבר כפר סמיר) שמנוהל על-ידי חברה קדישא חיפה. שניהם קבורים בחלקה ו, שורה 22 — דוד בקבר 102ד, לוסיה בקבר הצמוד 102ג, התבנית הסטנדרטית של זוגות בישראל.

sections.intro_en: Documented living descendants of Eliezer Griffel + Sarah Matel Chajes — i.e., they share Dov Rapaport's great-great-grandparents. These are not distant theory — they are findable, often on LinkedIn or with public author pages, and several have published memoirs about the same Galicia world. Reaching out to them could yield family photographs, oral history, and unpublished correspondence about Memek and Rivka.
sections.intro_he: צאצאים חיים מתועדים של אליעזר גריפל ושרה מאטל חיות — הם חולקים את אבות-הסבים של דב רפפורט. אינם קרובים תיאורטיים — ניתן למצוא אותם בפועל, חלקם בלינקדאין או בדפי מחברים פומביים, וכמה מהם פרסמו ספרי זיכרונות על אותה גליציה. פנייה אליהם עשויה להניב צילומי משפחה, היסטוריה בעל-פה והתכתבויות לא-פורסמות על ממק ורבקה.

cards.title_en: Dr Steven Lamm, MD — NYC (2nd cousin once removed)

Author of The Hardness Factor, No Guts No Glory, The Virility Solution. Regular Today Show medical contributor in the 2000s. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Zissel Griffel-Lamm → Arnold Lamm → Steven Lamm.
cards.summary_he: יליד 1948. NYU Langone Health, מנהטן — מנהל רפואי של מרכז Preston Robert Tisch לבריאות הגבר. מחבר ספרים רפואיים. שושלת: אליעזר גריפל → זיסל גריפל-לאם → ארנולד לאם → סטיבן לאם.

cards.title_en: Andrew Griffel — NYC / Israel (2nd cousin once removed)

cards.summary_he: עורך דין בינלאומי, לשעבר שופט בית דין צבאי. ניצול שואה 'ילד מוסתר' (הוריו ניצלו בפולין). מבלוג Times of Israel. שושלת: אליעזר גריפל → יצחק חיים גריפל → יהושע השל 'הנרי' גריפל → אנדרו.

cards.summary_en: Edward Gelles's autosomal-DNA-confirmed cousin in Israel. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Chaya Griffel-Gruenfeld → Sima Gruenfeld-Blau → Clara Blau-Heyn → Pinhas.

cards.summary_en: Named explicitly in Edward Gelles's Griffel of Nadworna PDF as "my son Haim, his spouse Shevi, and Avi". Contact via Balliol Archives (archives@balliol.ox.ac.uk).

cards.title_en: Boruch Griffel — Jerusalem
cards.summary_en: Son of Shmuel Shmelke Griffel (1900-?, brother of Dr Jacob Griffel the Vaad ha-Hatzala rescuer). Named by Edward Gelles as a private-communication source.
cards.summary_he: בן שמואל שמלקה גריפל (יליד 1900, אחיו של ד"ר יעקב גריפל המציל מטעם ועד ההצלה). מוזכר על-ידי אדוארד גלס כמקור התכתבות פרטית. גר בירושלים.

cards.title_en: Yehuda Nir (1930-2014) — author of The Lost Childhood (1st cousin once removed)
cards.summary_en: Born Juliusz Gruenfeld in Lwów 1930, died NYC 2014. Holocaust survivor, Cornell Professor of Psychiatry, author of the 1989 memoir The Lost Childhood — about surviving in Aryan-side Warsaw and Lwów with false papers (same world as Lusia's wartime experience). His mother Sima Blau (1895-1990) and sister Lala also survived. Line: Eliezer Griffel → Chaya Griffel-Gruenfeld → Samuel Gruenfeld → Yehuda. Required reading.
cards.summary_he: נולד יוליוס גרינפלד בלבוב 1930, נפטר ניו יורק 2014. ניצול שואה, פרופ' לפסיכיאטריה בקורנל, מחבר הספר 'הילדות האבודה' (1989) — על הישרדות בצד הארי בוורשה ולבוב עם מסמכים מזויפים (אותו עולם של חוויית לוסיה במלחמה). אמו סימה בלאו ואחותו לאלה גם הן ניצלו. שושלת: אליעזר גריפל → חיה גריפל-גרינפלד → שמואל גרינפלד → יהודה.

cards.title_en: 🟢 Edward Gelles — London (age 98, LIVING, the senior living expert on our family)
cards.title_he: 🟢 אדוארד גלס — לונדון (בן 98, חי, המומחה החי הבכיר על משפחתנו)
cards.summary_en: Born Vienna 24 November 1927; lives in London. 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek Rapaport. The published genealogist whose Balliol College Oxford archive documents the entire Griffel-Chajes-Wahl-Gelles dynasty. His mother Regina Gelles née Griffel was David Memek's first cousin. Authored 'An Ancient Lineage: European Roots of a Jewish Family' (2006). Knows everyone on the maternal line personally. THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT CONTACT FOR DORON.

cards.source: Edward Gelles, Griffel of Nadworna PDF + Balliol Archives

cards.title_en: 🟢 Tali Griffel Ginsburg — NYC (Ras Burqa attack survivor, 3rd cousin)
cards.summary_en: LIVING — NYC. 3rd cousin of David Memek Rapaport. Daughter of Andrew Griffel. Survivor of the Ras Burqa terror attack 5 October 1985 in Sinai — her mother Anita Spindel Griffel was murdered shielding 5-year-old Tali with her body. Andrew raised her in NYC. Now married, mother. Contributor to Tablet Magazine and Israel Story podcast (episode 'Thicker Than Water').

David Nir is Political Director of Daily Kos. Daniel and Aaron are private investor and fashion executive respectively (from Yehuda's first marriage). All 2nd cousins once removed of David Memek. cards.summary_en: Born 1953. LIVING. Only surviving child of Dr Jacob Griffel (1900-1962, the Vaad ha-Hatzala Istanbul rescuer of Polish Jews during WWII). Jacob's first wife and children perished in the Warsaw blitzkrieg; Joseph from Jacob's second marriage to Miriam Rottenberg (1914-1998). 2nd cousin once removed of David Memek.

cards.title_en: Diana Margaret Griffel — London (b. 27 Oct 1943, status uncertain)
cards.summary_en: Born London 27 October 1943. Daughter of Edward Griffel (David Memek's uncle) + Susan Manson. Status uncertain — would be 82. 2nd cousin of David Memek. Worth a targeted UK search.

cards.title_en: 🟢 Jackie Boehme Griffel — Rockville MD (Eric Griffel's widow)
cards.summary_en: LIVING — Rockville Maryland area. Widow of Eric Griffel (1930-2020), Edward Gelles's first cousin and USAID Pakistan Station Chief who signed the famous 'Blood Telegram' protesting US policy during the 1971 Bangladesh genocide. Contact via USAID Alumni Association obituary page.

cards.title_en: 🟢 The Tripcovich family of Trieste — Italian shipping aristocracy
cards.summary_en: Branch via Rachel Griffel + Bonya Ohrenstein → Lucia Ohrenstein (1910-1988) who became Countess Tripcovich, marrying Italian shipping magnate Oliviero Tripcovich (later Romeo Puri Purini and Count Livio Tripcovich). Italian descendants in Trieste — Diodato Tripcovich named. Findable via Trieste Chamber of Commerce / Italian shipping history archives.

sections.title_he: אילן יוחסין רבני-מסחרי בן 350 שנה (קו האם)
sections.intro_en: Through Sarah Matel Chajes of Kolomea — David Memek's grandmother — the Rapaport family connects to a documented multi-century rabbinical-merchant dynasty of Galicia.
sections.intro_he: דרך שרה מאטל חיות מקולומיאה — סבתו של דוד ממק — משפחת רפפורט מתחברת לשושלת רבנית-מסחרית מתועדת של גליציה.

cards.title_en: 🕍 The Rapoport-Kohen rabbinical dynasty (15th c. → today)
cards.title_he: 🕍 שושלת רפפורט-הכהן הרבנית (מאה 15 → היום)
cards.summary_en: The Rapoport family is one of the most famous Ashkenazi Kohanic rabbinical dynasties. Origin: R. Meshullam Yekutiel ha-Kohen Rapa (d.1450, Mainz → Porto, Italy). The surname "Rapaport" combines "Rapa" (the original Italian family name) with "Porto" (where the family fled and resettled). Members of the dynasty have served as Chief Rabbis across central Europe.
cards.quote_en: The family came from Mainz to Porto, Italy. R. Yekutiel ha-Kohen Rapa is the progenitor; his descendants added "Porto" to the name in commemoration of their new home. Major Galician branch: R. Abraham Rapoport "Schrenzel" of Lemberg (1584-1651), head of Lemberg yeshiva for 45 years, son-in-law of R. Mordecai Schrenzel, President of the Council of Four Lands. Lemberg / Cracow / Brody / Dubno / Krzemeniec lines descend from him.
cards.source: Wikipedia: Rapoport family + Jewish Encyclopedia (1906) entry on Rapoport

cards.title_en: What being a Kohen means today
cards.title_he: המשמעות של היותנו כהנים כיום
cards.summary_en: As Kohanim (descendants of Aaron the High Priest), the male Rapaports — Dov, Shimon, Doron, Daniel, and their sons (Ronen, Yarden, Itamar, Rom) — carry priestly status. In traditional Jewish practice this means: called first to read from the Torah (Aliyah Rishona), eligible to perform the Priestly Blessing (Birkat Kohanim / Duchanen), restricted from entering cemeteries (except for immediate family), restricted from marrying a divorcée or convert (in strictly Orthodox practice). The family does NOT use "Cohen" as a surname — "Rapaport" IS the name that carries the Kohanic identity, by ancient family tradition.
cards.summary_he: כצאצאי אהרן הכהן, גברי רפפורט — דב, שמעון, דורון, דניאל ובניהם (רונן, ירדן, איתמר, רום) — נושאים מעמד כהני. במסורת היהודית: קוראים ראשונים בתורה (עליה ראשונה), זכאים לברכת כהנים (דוכן), מנועים מלהיכנס לבית קברות (פרט לקרובי משפחה מדרגה ראשונה), מנועים מלשאת גרושה או גיורת (לפי הלכה אורתודוקסית קפדנית). המשפחה לא נושאת את השם 'כהן' — 'רפפורט' הוא השם הנושא את הזהות הכהנית, על-פי מסורת משפחתית עתיקה.

cards.title_en: Chajes-Halevi Segal-Sirkes rabbinical line
cards.title_he: שושלת חיות-הלוי סגל-סירקיש הרבנית
cards.summary_en: Sarah Matel Chajes descended from Isaac Chaim Chayes (1823-1866) of Kolomea, who descended from Isaac ben Abraham Chayes (1538-1617), Chief Rabbi of Prague. Through this line the family connects to David Halevi Segal (the "Taz"), Chief Rabbi of Lvov, and Joel Sirkes (the "Bach"), Chief Rabbi of Cracow.
cards.summary_he: שרה מאטל חיות צאצאית של יצחק חיים חיות (1823-1866) מקולומיאה, צאצא של יצחק בן אברהם חיות (1538-1617), הרב הראשי של פראג. דרך שושלת זו המשפחה מתחברת לדוד הלוי סגל ('הט"ז'), הרב הראשי של לבוב, וליואל סירקיש ('הב"ח'), הרב הראשי של קרקוב.
cards.source: Edward Gelles, Facets of My Family History

cards.title_en: The family were Hager-Ottynia Chasidim
cards.title_he: המשפחה הייתה חסידי האגר-אוטיניה
cards.summary_en: Eliezer Griffel and his sons + sons-in-law (including Berisz Rapaport) "prayed regularly in the synagogue of the Rabbi of nearby Ottynia" — the Hager Chasidic dynasty of Ottynia.
cards.summary_he: אליעזר גריפל ובניו וחתניו (כולל בריש רפפורט) 'התפללו באופן קבוע בבית הכנסת של הרב הסמוך באוטיניה' — חצר חסידות האגר מאוטיניה.
cards.source: Edward Gelles, Facets of My Family History citing Nadwórna Memorial Book p.26

sections.intro_en: Through the Griffel-Wahl marriage (David Mendel Griffel + Chawa Wahl ~1896), the documented Katzenellenbogen rabbinical dynasty of Padua-Venice entered the family tree. Chawa Wahl (1877-1941, perished Holocaust Nadwórna) was a direct descendant of Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen (1541-1617) — the Polish-Jewish merchant whom legend remembers as 'King of Poland for one night' between Stefan Báthory's death and Sigismund III Vasa's election. This connection runs through the cousin line (Edward Gelles, Steven Lamm, Andrew Griffel, the Nirs, Pinhas Heyn, Boruch Griffel etc.) — all carry both the Chajes pedigree (David Memek's maternal grandmother) and Wahl-Katzenellenbogen pedigree (David Mendel Griffel's wife Chawa).

cards.title_en: Saul Wahl Katzenellenbogen (1541-1617) — 'King of Poland for one night'
cards.summary_en: Polish-Jewish merchant. Married Deborah Rivkah Drucker (a relative of Moses Isserles). According to Jewish legend served as 'rex pro tempore' of Poland on 18 August 1587 between Stefan Báthory's death and Sigismund III Vasa's election. Some traditions say he ruled one night only; others a few days. The surname 'Wahl' comes from German 'wahl' = 'election'. 13 children. Y-DNA-confirmed ancestor of multiple modern descendants including Edward Gelles.
cards.source: Wikipedia + Jewish Encyclopedia + Edward Gelles 'WAHL of Tarnobrzeg' (Balliol)

cards.title_en: The Katzenellenbogen Padua-Venice dynasty (1482-1597)
cards.summary_en: R. Meir Katzenellenbogen 'MaHaRaM Padua' (c.1482-1565) — Chief Rabbi of Padua; mother Julia-Malka Luria descended from Rashi via daughter Miriam; married Hannah granddaughter of Judah Minz. His son R. Samuel Judah Katzenellenbogen (1521-1597) became Chief Rabbi of Venice. Samuel's son was Saul Wahl. Three documented generations of Italian rabbinical leadership before the family moved into Polish-Lithuanian Jewry.

cards.title_en: Tarnobrzeg Wahls → Shulim Wahl → Chawa Wahl Griffel (1877-1941)
cards.summary_en: The chain from Meir Wahl 'MaHaRaSH' (c.1565-1631, ABD Brest, m. Hinde Horowitz of Cracow) down through 17th-19th-century Galician Wahl/Wohl families in Cracow + Tarnobrzeg → Shulim Wahl + Sarah Safier of Tarnobrzeg → Chawa Wahl (1877-1941) who married David Mendel Griffel of Nadwórna in ~1896. Children: Zygmunt (1897-1951), Regina (1900-1954, mother of Edward Gelles), Edward (1904-1959). Documented by Edward Gelles in 'WAHL of Tarnobrzeg and WOHL of Cracow' (Balliol).
cards.source: Edward Gelles, 'WAHL of Tarnobrzeg and WOHL of Cracow' (Balliol College Oxford archives)

cards.title_en: 🧬 Y-DNA confirmation — Katzenellenbogen rabbinical lineage (Avotaynu 2016)
cards.summary_en: Avotaynu Online published 'The Y-DNA Genetic Signature and Ethnic Origin of the Katzenellenbogen Rabbinical Lineage' (2016). Confirms the Saul Wahl line has documented Y-DNA. Edward Gelles tested his own DNA — the result connects him to both the Wahl-Katzenellenbogen line and Sephardic Benveniste descendants.

cards.title_en: Zaynvl Rapaport — Bolechów timber specialist

cards.quote_en: In the management of the big factories were Jews, who were doing their task their whole life, from their youth. They were engaged in the construction of the factory's building from the very beginning. In the surroundings they were well known as specialists in the wood-profession. The following should be mentioned: Mr. (Reb) Moyshe Bin, who emigrated to Israel and died here; Zaynvl Rapaport; Aharon Shor and the Shtrasman-brothers.

cards.title_en: ✅ Zygmunt Griffel burial CONFIRMED — Baron Hirsch Staten Island, YMBA Section I Gate 51
cards.title_he: ✅ קבר זיגמונט גריפל אומת — בית הקברות באראן הירש סטטן איילנד
cards.summary_en: Eric Griffel's 2015 ADST testimony is now confirmed by primary source. His father Zygmunt Griffel (1897-1951) is documented in Ada Green's transcription of the Nadworner Young Men's Benevolent Association burial register at Baron Hirsch Cemetery, 1126 Richmond Avenue, Staten Island NY — Section I, Gate 51. Died 16 May 1951, age 53. Edward Griffel (1904-1959) and Susan Manson Griffel (d.1978) are buried in the same plot. Bernard Griffel (1899-1962) + Mollie Mehrbaum (1890-1966) are in nearby Section A, Gate 113. Eric's quote 'on Staten Island in the Nadworna Jewish émigré community organization plot' is documentarily verified.
cards.source: Ada Green's transcription of Nadworner landsmanshaft burials, JewishGen KehilaLinks

cards.title_en: 🎯 YIVO RG 1622 — First Nadworna SBA archive (1897-1984), holds Zygmunt's plot file
cards.title_he: 🎯 YIVO RG 1622 — ארכיון אגודת נדבורנה הראשונה (1897-1984)
cards.summary_en: YIVO Archives RG 1622 holds the complete records of the First Nadworna Sick Benevolent Association (1897-1984): Constitution 1897 (English); Membership books 1900-1963 (German); Income & expense journals 1935-1984; Members' cemetery plot reservation files at Baron de Hirsch; Correspondence with Baron de Hirsch Cemetery. This is where Zygmunt Griffel's ORIGINAL 1951 plot-reservation paperwork lives — plus likely his membership card with parents' names + dates of birth + dates of immigration. Single most valuable original-document target for the Griffel-Nadwórna branch.

cards.title_en: 🎯 JDC Cyprus name index — single most actionable database (16,667 pages indexed)
cards.title_he: 🎯 אינדקס שמות JDC קפריסין — המסד הזמין ביותר לפעולה
cards.summary_en: The Joint Distribution Committee (JDC) Cyprus Collection is the single most actionable online database for finding our family. It consists of 16,667 pages of textual files digitized from 19 microfilm reels. Quote from JDC topic guide: 'births to Cyprus detainees from 1948-1949 are documented, and there are indexed lists that reveal genealogical information such as names, age, date of birth, place of birth, nationality, group affiliation, family status, and even weight.' A family of three with David b.1911-12-25, Lusia b.1913-04-08, Shimon b.1937-06-22 should be locatable via DOB cross-match alone.

cards.title_en: 🎯 Haifa Chevra Kadisha — 2 entries for 'דוד רפפורט' may include David Memek
cards.title_he: 🎯 חברה קדישא חיפה — 2 רישומים ל'דוד רפפורט', אולי דוד ממק
cards.summary_en: The Haifa Chevra Kadisha (Jewish burial society) has indexable entries for at least two 'דוד רפפורט'. Their entries typically list Hebrew date of death + birth year + parents' names — the single most likely place to nail David Memek's exact death date. The two URLs to check manually: deceased ID 10700 (memorial info page) + deceased ID 148324 (funeral info page).

cards.title_en: 🎯 DAIFO R-432/1/1 — Hans Krueger's Nadwórna execution orders Oct-Nov 1941
cards.title_he: 🎯 ДАІФО ר-432/1/1 — פקודות ההוצאה להורג של הנס קרוגר נדבורנה אוקטובר-נובמבר 1941
cards.summary_en: Hans Krueger was the SD-Hauptsturmführer overseeing the destruction of Jewish life in Bezirk Stanislau (incl. Nadwórna). DAIFO fond R-432, opys 1, sprava 1 contains his execution orders for 13 October – 7 November 1941, the immediate aftermath of the 6 October 1941 great Aktion. The village Zelenoe (Зелена, near Nadwórna) is named. This fond may name Berisz Rapaport and Rebeka Griffel-Rapaport among the executed — the strongest hope of confirming their exact fate.
cards.source: DAIFO (State Archive of Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast) — Yad Vashem academic article on Krueger

cards.title_en: 🎯 Brussels Aliens Police dossier — David + Lusia + Shimon (guaranteed)
cards.title_he: 🎯 תיקי משטרת הזרים בבריסל — דוד, לוסיה ושמעון (מובטח)
cards.summary_en: Archives générales du Royaume Brussels holds 2 million+ Aliens Police individual dossiers. David, Lusia and Shimon each have a dossier from their April 1946 Brussels arrival — guaranteed. Will contain photo, civil-status documents, employment declaration, addresses 1946-47, exit notice on departure via Sète April 1947. Expected to contain David's first written wartime narrative naming his murdered family.

cards.title_en: 🎯 Landesarchiv Berlin — David's Wiedergutmachung file
cards.summary_en: Berlin Senate decided early 2026 to transfer 250,000-280,000 BEG individual files (1953-1969) from LABO to the Landesarchiv. David almost certainly has a BEG file — the memoir explicitly states he and Lusia used the German reparations payments to buy their Haifa apartment on Moriah Street 93. Expected contents: persecution narrative (in David's hand, German or Hebrew), witness affidavits, photo, payment records.

cards.title_en: 🎯 Arolsen 7-7-2_7734344 — Personal file of WEITZNER Léa (2 pages)
cards.title_he: 🎯 ארולסן 7-7-2_7734344 — תיק אישי וייצנר לאה (2 עמודים)

cards.title_en: Stanisławów Jewish Workers Registration Cards 1941-42 (USHMM, indexed on Ancestry)
cards.title_he: כרטיסי רישום עובדים יהודים סטניסלבוב 1941-42 (USHMM, באינדקס Ancestry)
cards.summary_en: USHMM-donated collection of Social Insurance Office cards for Jewish workers during Nazi occupation 1941-42, administering Nadwórna among others. The closest surviving analogue to David's wartime work permit registration.

cards.title_en: 🌲 Foresta company — David Memek's wartime sawmill (Jewish-owned + Aryanised 1941)
cards.summary_en: Foresta is confirmed in three independent sources (Yad Vashem, Pinkas Hakehillot Polin, Jewish Galicia & Bukovina) as the Jewish-owned, Jewish-managed Nadwórna timber operation. Leased state woodlands from the Polish authorities, exported timber. Contract cancelled 1937 under antisemitic pressure. Nationalised by Soviets after September 1939 — Jews retained managerial positions. Under German occupation 1941-42, approximately 600 Jews worked daily at the sawmill (= Foresta) to obtain 'essential worker' permits. David Memek Rapaport's wartime work permit was for this operation. Owners' individual names are NOT on the public web — they're in the full Hebrew Pinkas Hakehillot Polin text and likely in the 1975 Carmi Nadwórna Yizkor book.

sections.intro_en: 26 critical pages of the memoir were re-translated with Claude Sonnet 4.5 and compared to the original Haiku 4.5 translation. New facts surfaced.
sections.intro_he: 26 עמודים קריטיים של היומן תורגמו מחדש על-ידי Claude Sonnet 4.5 והושוו לתרגום המקורי של Haiku 4.5. עלו עובדות חדשות.

cards.title_en: New person: "Doner" (p.48) — likely German employer surname
cards.summary_en: On p.48 of the memoir a German/Austrian surname "Doner" appears, possibly the supervisor or shop employer Lusia worked under while using her false identity.

cards.title_en: New person: Kramer the dentist from Nadwórna (p.48) — murdered after gold found in his home
cards.summary_en: p.48 mentions a Kramer dentist from Nadwórna whose daughter Lusia encountered in Lwów. The dentist was killed by the Germans after gold was found in his home. A community member, not direct family.

cards.title_en: Biblical reference identified: 1 Kings 21:19 (p.7)
cards.summary_en: Sonnet identified that the Hebrew "הרצחת וגם ירשת?!" on p.7 is a direct quote from 1 Kings 21:19 — Elijah's accusation to Ahab after the theft of Naboth's vineyard: "Have you murdered and also inherited?" Lusia uses this as moral indictment of the Nazis.

cards.title_en: A second Shimon (NOT Lusia's son) appears p.45 — hidden in a wooden crate
cards.summary_en: p.45 mentions a different Jewish child named Shimon hidden by his mother (not Lusia) in a wooden crate 70 cm × 1.50 m, in the millinery shop where Lusia worked. Important to distinguish from Lusia's son Shimon b.1937.

cards.title_en: Two Lwów place names need identification: "Yaichor" + "Yeplatsovka"
cards.summary_en: p.56 mentions "Yaichor" (ייחור) — possibly a small dwelling type — and "Yeplatsovka" (יפלאצבקה) — a place name near the Lwów market. Possibly Polish street/neighbourhood names like "Pełczyńska". Worth cross-referencing against 1939 Lwów street directories.

sections.intro_en: Where the memoir disagrees with the historical record, we preserve Lusia's testimony but attach a respectful historical note.
sections.intro_he: כאשר היומן חולק על הרשומה ההיסטורית, אנו שומרים את עדותה של לוסיה אך מצרפים הערה היסטורית מכובדת.

cards.title_en: p.78 — Lusia's visit to "Hitler's grave" in East Berlin (~1958)
cards.title_he: עמ' 78 — ביקור לוסיה ב'קבר היטלר' במזרח ברלין (~1958)
cards.summary_en: The memoir describes Lusia visiting a stone in East Berlin marked "Hitler" and spitting on it to close the circle of her past. Historically, Hitler committed suicide April 30 1945 in the Führerbunker; his and Eva Braun's remains were cremated by Soviet SMERSH, with final bone fragments destroyed by the KGB at Magdeburg in 1970. No publicly-known grave bearing his name has ever existed. Preserved as Lusia's lived experience — perhaps a different memorial misremembered, perhaps a propaganda site shown by East German authorities, perhaps a memory reconstruction across decades.
cards.summary_he: היומן מתאר ביקור של לוסיה אצל אבן במזרח ברלין הנושאת את הכתובת 'היטלר', שירקה עליה כדי לסגור את מעגל עברה. היסטורית, היטלר התאבד ב-30.4.1945 בבונקר; שרידיו נשרפו על-ידי הסמרש הסובייטי, פירורי העצם האחרונים הושמדו ב-1970 במגדבורג. אין שום קבר שנשא את שמו. נשמר כעדותה של לוסיה — אולי זיהוי מוטעה של מצבה אחרת, אולי אתר תעמולה שהציגו השלטונות הקומוניסטיים, אולי שחזור זיכרון לאחר עשורים.

sections.intro_en: Concrete next steps Doron can take. Each is a specific request to a specific institution.
sections.intro_he: צעדים קונקרטיים שדורון יכול לבצע. כל פריט הוא בקשה ספציפית למוסד ספציפי.

cards.summary_en: The Balliol PDFs almost certainly name David "Memek" Rapaport explicitly within the Eliezer Griffel 10-child family tree. Read the index PDF first, then jump to every "Rapaport" page reference.

cards.title_en: Run All-Galicia Database searches for all four surnames
cards.summary_en: Gesher Galicia's All Galicia DB has 2,833 Rapaport records + indexed Nadwórna + Bolechów. Search Rapaport+Nadwórna, Griffel+Nadwórna, Weitzner+Bolechów, Weinreb+Dolina. Save every source code.

cards.title_en: Run Arolsen Archives search for Rapaport / Weitzner
cards.summary_en: Arolsen Archives indexes DP camp registrations, Care & Maintenance forms, tracing requests, Arbeitskarte fragments. Search David Mendel Rapaport b.25.12.1911 Nadwórna; Lusia Weitzner b.8.4.1913 Bolechów; Shimon Rapaport b.1937.
